title: Leisure duty manager
change: Occupational standard and assessment plan revised
overviewOfRole: Managing the day to day operation of a leisure facility.

knowledges.detail: Policies and processes for opening and closing a leisure facility, including out of hours incident management.

Operational processes and procedures for making specialist areas of a leisure facility available for use. For example, reception, swimming pools, fitness areas, sports halls, plant rooms, changing rooms and activity areas.

The processes involved in the safe preparation and resetting of key areas, including ad hoc bookings and associated equipment. For example, dry side facilities, the swimming pool environment, trampolines and electronic accessible hoists.
Equipment inspection techniques including maintenance schedules and reporting.
Organisational and legislative requirements for preparing for and managing large gatherings and events.
Communication techniques to ensure a high level of interaction with staff, customers, and visitors such as contractors and clients.
Approaches to managing teams and performance. For example, coaches, lifeguards, contractors and self-employed staff.
Relevant regulation, legislation, and compliance and how it impacts their role and the organisation. For example, GDPR, ethical, environmental and social policies, safeguarding and Natasha’s law.
Critical Incident management processes including recovery programme, business continuity and wider community crisis support.

Managing organisational processes and procedures for recruitment, induction, staff training, mandatory and regulated continued professional development including the importance of safeguarding checks.

Customer profiles within the local community and how they impact the approach within the facility and the service expectation from the specific customer group for example women only sessions, disabilities and religious beliefs.
The organisation's customer service standards, service level agreements, feedback processes and the digital systems that support them.

How to manage Health and Safety in accordance with legal and employer requirements for the safe running of a leisure facility for all customers and staff. For example, pool plant operations, chemicals and hazardous substances, risk assessments, manual handling, and first aid.

The organisation's environmental procedures and how they may impact day to day leisure operations. For example, energy conservation and reduction, minimising waste and recyclable controls regarding financial and moral responsibility.
Quality assurance process and procedures to deliver a high-quality functioning facility that supports customers wellbeing.

The business brand and values of the organisation alongside upholding its reputation to support business goals and service targets through its operations, products and services to its community and customers on offer.

The purpose of their role within the organisation, their level of responsibility and accountability. For example, when procuring the services of contractors and suppliers or issuing refunds to customers.

The organisation's central management systems and its components such as customer database, sports activity bookings, staff rota systems and daily and weekly management reports and contingency plans for non-operation.
Revenue and protection during shifts including all cash handling procedures. For example, till reconciliation, vending machines and catering outlets.
Stock control methods for items such as saleable goods, membership vouchers, cleaning and pool chemicals and event tickets.
Principles of financial monitoring and management and how they impact on the organisation's financial performance.
The importance of the industries contribution to the health and wellbeing of the communities.
Methods to drive revenue within the facility. For example, secondary spend, upselling and utilisation of unused space.
Approaches to promotion and publicity of the facility to increase engagement with the local community. For example, the use of digital display screens and marketing promotions.

behaviours.detail: Takes ownership of own work, explains decisions and stands by them when required.
Considers the environment, sustainability and energy consumption when using resources and carrying out work.
Acts in a professional and ethical manner, embracing equity, diversity and inclusion in the workplace.
Team focused and works effectively with colleagues and others.

skills.detail: Open and close a leisure facility in line with organisation and health and safety requirements.
Ensure facility is fully operational, resourced and prepared for customer use. For example, equipment, sports halls, gyms, outdoor pitches and swimming pools.
Solve problems to ensure customer service standards are met and maintained.
Support recruitment and induction of new staff, contractors and volunteers including conducting all necessary legal and organisational checks. For example, DBS checks.
Plan, organise and manage the allocation and rotation of staff, ensuring compliance with organisational and industry policies and procedures. For example, safe staffing levels and ratios.
Lead team in emergency or business continuity situations in accordance with facilities emergency action plan.
Use coaching and motivational techniques to support team performance, engagement and development.
Share positive feedback, success and key learnings in a timely manner ensuring all staff adhere to agreed processes and procedures.
Adapt own leadership style to different situations and audiences.
Support the facility management team with actioning HR processes. For example, talent, probation, performance, disciplinary or sickness management.
Manage customer comments and complaints following organisational procedures, including recommending improvements where necessary to prevent any reoccurrence of problems.
Perform regular audits and assessments to monitor and review facility compliance with legislation and industry guidance. For example, fire exit checks, changing room cleanliness, air temperatures.
Set up and use equipment and materials and as per manufacturers guidance.
Promote the business brand by using of marketing resources and tone of voice used by the organisation.
Collate and interpret data and information for the facility manager on measures such as staff issues, customer complaints, events, incidents and accidents and make recommendations where required.
Manage and monitor budgets, targets and financial key performance indicators in own area of responsibility.

Use central management systems to support the effectiveness of the organisation and facility. For example, building management systems, customer relations management systems, labour scheduling tools and electronic passes.
Monitor and audit stock control systems.

reviewDetails: this apprenticeship will be reviewed in accordance with our change request policy.
clusterDescription: Co-ordination, management and delivery of specific business processes and also their direct provision to users

qualifications.title: Active IQ Level 3 Certificate in Leisure  Management

professionalRecognition.bodyName: Chartered Institute for the Management of Sport and Physical Activity (CIMSPA)

duties.dutyDetail: Ensure the safety of the site, facilities, equipment, plant, staff and customers including ensuring that the site and facilities meet the highest standards of health and safety legislation and regulation.
Lead the response to critical incident management, reporting for both employees and the general public.
Manage all areas of the facility ensuring the highest possible standards of cleanliness, security and operational effectiveness and efficiency.
Manage, allocate, supervise, train and motivate operational staff whilst on shift.
Ensure activity areas are prepared for use in accordance with the planned programme and ad hoc bookings. Making sure relevant health and safety standards are met.
Ensure staff and customers comply with the employer’s policies, procedures and standards
Manage the customer experience, including dealing with all customer complaints and customer service enquiries.

Monitor quality and function of key systems within the facility. For example, ensuring building management systems are functioning, pool plant or access systems are operating according to manufacturer, legislative and regulatory requirements.
Ensure compliance with organisational, environmental, ethical, social and sustainability strategies and policies.
Ensure the smooth and secure opening and closing of a facility and responding to out of hours incidents in accordance with policies and procedures.
Manage the daily financial procedures within the facility. For example, reconciling tills, safe contents and coordinating secure cash collection.
Support health and wellbeing initiatives within the centre and/or local communities, with a focus on social impact, social mobility and engagement.
Assist in the promotion of the facility and the services it offers.
